text: A 6×6 matrix A is formed where each element independently follows Bernoulli(p = 0.4). The probability that both the sum of the 3rd row equals 3 AND the sum of the 4th column equals 2 is ________. (Rounded off to four decimal places)
explanation:
•	Row sum = 3: C(6,3)(0.4)³(0.6)³
	•	Column sum = 2: C(6,2)(0.4)²(0.6)⁴
	•	Since independent: multiply = 0.2765 × 0.0498 = 0.0138

text:
For a dataset {y₁, y₂, ..., y₈₀} where n = 80, it is given that:
(1/12800) ∑ᵢ₌₁⁸⁰ ∑ⱼ₌₁⁸⁰ (yᵢ - yⱼ)² = 144
The value of the sample variance s² = (1/79)∑ᵢ₌₁⁸⁰(yᵢ - ȳ)² is ________. (Answer in integer)

Seven distinct books are arranged on a shelf. Books B₁ and B₂ must not be adjacent, and among books B₃, B₄, B₅, the relative order must be B₃-B₄-B₅ (not necessarily consecutive). The number of valid arrangements is:
From the set S = {1, 2, 3, ..., 60}, three distinct numbers are chosen uniformly at random. The probability that their sum is divisible by 4 is ________. (Rounded off to three decimal places)

Twelve identical red balls and eight identical blue balls are to be distributed into 4 distinct boxes such that each box contains at least one ball of each color. The number of ways to do this is ________. (Answer in integer)
explanation:
•	Stars and bars with constraints
	•	Red: 12 balls, 4 boxes, min 1 each = C(11,3) = 165
	•	Blue: 8 balls, 4 boxes, min 1 each = C(7,3) = 35
	•	35

text: A committee of 7 is chosen from 10 men and 8 women. The probability that the committee contains at least 3 men AND at least 2 women is:

A non-empty subset M is chosen uniformly at random from all subsets of {2, 3, 5, 7, 11, 13}. The probability that the product of all elements in M is even is ________. (Rounded off to three decimal places)
In a sequence of n = 6 Bernoulli trials with p = 0.5, let A be the event "at least one success" and B be the event "at least two consecutive successes". Then P(B | A) equals:
explanation:
•	P(A) = 1 - (1/2)⁶ = 63/64
	•	P(B∩A) = Count sequences with at least one HH / 64
	•	P(B|A) = 27/63

text: Eight people including 3 married couples are seated around a circular table. The probability that no married couple sits together (adjacent seats) is ________. (Rounded off to four decimal places)

Five letters addressed to five different people are randomly placed in five envelopes (one letter per envelope). The probability that at least one letter is in the correct envelope is ________. (Rounded off to three decimal places)
explanation:
•	P(at least one correct) = 1 - P(none correct)
	•	P(derangement) = D₅/5! = 44/120
	•	P(at least one) = 76/120 = 0.633

text: From a group of 15 students (8 boys, 7 girls), 3 students are selected for Stage 1. From these 3, 2 are selected for Stage 2. The probability that both Stage 2 selections are boys, given that Stage 1 had at least 2 boys, is:

Ten distinct balls are placed into 4 distinct boxes such that the first box contains exactly 3 balls. The number of ways this can be done is ________. (Answer in integer, divide by 1000 and round to nearest integer)
A random experiment consists of selecting a random subset A from the power set of {1,2,3,4,5,6} (including empty set and full set). Which of the following statements is/are correct?

options.text: P(sum of elements in A is even) = 1/2

text: Six cards numbered 1 to 6 are randomly placed in six positions numbered 1 to 6. The expected number of matches (card i in position i) is ________. (Answer in integer)
Events A₁, A₂, A₃ are mutually independent with P(A₁) = P(A₂) = P(A₃) = 0.3. The probability that exactly one of these events occurs is:

Box X contains 5 red, 3 white balls. Box Y contains 2 red, 6 white balls. A fair die is rolled: if outcome ≤ 2, pick from X; if outcome ≥ 5, pick from Y; otherwise, pick from X with probability 0.6 and Y with probability 0.4. Given a red ball was picked, the probability the die showed exactly 3 is ________. (Rounded off to three decimal places)

A disease affects 3% of the population. Test T₁ has sensitivity 0.90 and specificity 0.85. Test T₂ has sensitivity 0.95 and specificity 0.80. A person takes both tests independently, and both are positive. The probability the person has the disease is:

Events A, B, C satisfy: P(A) = 0.5, P(B|A) = 0.6, P(B|Aᶜ) = 0.4, P(C|A∩B) = 0.7, P(C|A∩Bᶜ) = 0.3, P(C|Aᶜ∩B) = 0.5, P(C|Aᶜ∩Bᶜ) = 0.2. The value of P(A|C) is ________. (Rounded off to three decimal places)

Urn U₁ has 6 white, 4 black balls. Urn U₂ has 3 white, 7 black balls. Two balls are drawn from U₁ and placed in U₂, then one ball is drawn from U₂. The probability this final ball is white is ________. (Rounded off to three decimal places)

Machine M produces 70% of items, machine N produces 30%. From M, 5% are defective; from N, 10% are defective. An item is tested and found defective. A second item from the SAME machine is then selected. The probability this second item is also defective is:
Let P(A) = 0.6. Event B is such that P(B|A) = 2×P(B|Aᶜ) and P(A∪B) = 0.85. The value of P(B) is ________. (Rounded off to two decimal places)

Three urns contain white and black balls: U₁(4W, 2B), U₂(3W, 3B), U₃(2W, 4B). An urn is selected: P(U₁) = 0.5, P(U₂) = 0.3, P(U₃) = 0.2. Two balls are drawn without replacement from the selected urn, both are white. The probability that urn U₁ was selected is:

Random variable Y takes values {1, 2, 3} with P(Y=1) = 0.3, P(Y=2) = 0.5, P(Y=3) = 0.2. Given Y=y, event E occurs with probability y/6. Given that E occurs, the expected value of Y is ________. (Rounded off to two decimal places)
Events P and Q satisfy P(P) = 0.4, P(Q) = 0.5, P(P∩Q) = 0.15. The value of P(P|P∪Q) is ________. (Rounded off to three decimal places)

A medical test classifies patients as Low/Medium/High risk with probabilities 0.6/0.3/0.1 respectively. Actual disease rates are: Low=2%, Medium=8%, High=25%. A random patient tests positive (test sensitivity = 0.9 for all groups, specificity = 0.85 for all). The probability they are High risk is:

Box A has balls numbered {1,2,3}, Box B has balls numbered {2,4,6,8}. A fair coin is tossed: Heads→draw from A, Tails→draw from B. Let X be the number drawn. The value of Var(X) is ________. (Rounded off to two decimal places)
Random variables X, Y, Z satisfy: P(X,Y,Z) = P(X)P(Y|X)P(Z|Y). Which of the following is/are correct?

options.text: X and Z are conditionally independent given Y
X and Z are marginally independent
Y and Z are conditionally independent given X

text: Let X ~ Binomial(n=15, p=0.4). The probability P(4 ≤ X ≤ 8 | X ≥ 3) is ________. (Rounded off to three decimal places)
Let X ~ Binomial(n=200, p=0.015). Using Poisson approximation, the error |P_Binomial(X=5) - P_Poisson(X=5)| is closest to:

A biased coin with P(H) = 0.6 is tossed until exactly 4 heads appear. The expected total number of tosses is ________. (Rounded off to two decimal places)
A random variable Y has the following distribution: With probability 0.4, Y ~ Poisson(λ=2); with probability 0.6, Y ~ Poisson(λ=5). The value of E[Y²] is:

A deck of 52 cards (13 each of 4 suits) is shuffled. Five cards are drawn without replacement. The probability of getting exactly 2 spades AND exactly 2 hearts is ________. (Rounded off to three decimal places)
Let X be uniformly distributed over {1, 2, 3, ..., 20}. Define Y = ⌊X/3⌋ (floor function). Then E[Y] equals:

For X ~ Binomial(n=25, p), if the most likely value (mode) of X is 15, then p must satisfy:

Emails arrive according to a Poisson process with rate λ=6 per hour. Given that exactly 3 emails arrived in the first 30 minutes, the probability that exactly 2 of them arrived in the first 15 minutes is ________. (Rounded off to three decimal places)
Let X ~ Binomial(10, 0.5) and define Y = 10 - X. Then P(X + Y = 12) equals:

Let X ~ Poisson(λ). Which of the following is/are correct?

Let X ~ Normal(50, 25) and Y ~ Normal(45, 16) be independent. Then P(X > Y) equals:

Let Z ~ Normal(0,1). If P(Z < k) = 0.8, then P(|Z| < k) equals:

Let X ~ Normal(100, 100). Given that X > 100, the mean of X is:

Let X₁, X₂ ~ Exponential(λ=1) be independent. Then E[min(X₁, X₂)] equals ________. (Rounded off to two decimal places)

options.text: X and Y are not independent

text: Let X ~ Uniform[0,2] and Y ~ Uniform[0,2] be independent. Then P(X+Y ≤ 2) is ________. (Rounded off to three decimal places)

options.text: Var(Y) = Var(X) if g is linear

text: A random variable X has the property that E[X^n] = n! for all positive integers n. The probability P(X ≤ 2) is ________. (Rounded off to three decimal places) [Hint: Recognize the distribution]

END OF PRACTICE SET
Study Strategy:
	•	Master Q1-Q120 first (core patterns)
	•	Attempt T1-T20 only after 90%+ accuracy on main sections
	•	Time yourself: 2 minutes per 2-mark, 90 seconds per 1-mark
	•	Review wrong answers to identify pattern gaps
